data.title: Court Orders Yonyu Plastics Chairman to Return Seals in Y.Y. Cable Lawsuit
data.description:
Yonyu Plastics received a civil judgment from the Tainan District Court involving a lawsuit filed by Y.Y. Cable Accessories.

The court ordered the company chairman to return seals numbered 11 and 12, featuring the Wei-Cheng Wang design, to Y.Y. Cable, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

Yonyu Plastics has engaged a lawyer to handle the legal procedures and is considering whether to appeal, it noted.

data.title: Market Chatter: Singapore Premium Grocery Segment's Outlook Strengthens on Robust Spending Data, DBS Says
data.description:
DBS Group Research said consumers' growing preference for at-home meals bodes well for the long-term growth outlook of Singapore's premium grocery market, The Edge Singapore reported Wednesday.

The premium grocery sector has pointed to a rebound post-pandemic, supported by data showing higher spending per person among private households, the report cited DBS analysts as saying.

On the other hand, the food and beverage retail segment has weakened following the pandemic, the report cited the analysts as saying.

Dampened private consumption in China, a boost in foreign funding for Southeast Asian economies, and margin improvements due to normalizing commodity prices define the sector's near-term prospects, the report cited the equity research firm as saying.

DBS selected DFI Retail Group Holdings and Thai Beverage as its top picks in the sector, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Nordex to Deliver Turbines to Four Spanish Wind Farms
data.description:
Nordex secured orders to deliver wind turbines to four unnamed customers for wind farm projects totaling 259 megawatts in Spain.

The German wind power company will supply 27 N163/5.X, 13 N163/6.X, and two N149/5.X wind turbines, with service agreements of between 10 and 25 years, according to a Friday release.

Installation will commence in autumn and commissioning is expected to start by the end of 2025.

data.title: Singapore Central Bank Could Hold Fire Until Trump Policy Becomes More Clear — Market Talk
data.description: The Monetary Authority of Singapore is in no rush to ease monetary policy settings, CIMB analysts say, reiterating their call for a hold until April. Although inflation appears to be easing, the central bank will likely err on the side of caution, they write in a note. MAS may want to wait to assess the impact of U.S. President-elect Trump's policies first, they say. Significant trade, growth and inflation uncertainty stemming from Trump's policies could lead to some market volatility after his inauguration later this month, they say. Other events to look out for include Singapore's budget in February and geopolitical risks in Eastern Europe, the analysts add.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Clarkson Expects Results Ahead of Market Views
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

Clarkson said it expects full-year results for 2024 to be slightly ahead of current market expectations.

The London-listed provider of shipping services said Friday that underlying pretax profit--which strips out exceptional and other one-off items--is expected to be no less than 115 million pounds ($141.5 million) for 2024, subject to audit. Company-compiled market expectations for adjusted pretax profit are 111.3 million pounds.

For 2023, the company reported underlying pretax profit of 109.2 million pounds.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com

data.title: Vodafone Sells Remaining 3% Share in Indus Towers; Increases Stake in India's Vodafone Idea
data.description:
By Najat Kantouar

Vodafone Group sold its remaining 3% stake in telecom tower company Indus Towers and used the 28.0 billion Indian Rupees ($325.9 million) raised in proceeds to repay debt and increase its stake in India's Vodafone Idea.

The U.K. telecommunication group said Friday that it has completed the placing of its 79.2 million shares in Indus Towers via an accelerated bookbuild offering, as previously announced in December.

Part of the proceeds, representing 8.9 billion Indian Rupees, have been used to fully repay its existing debt secured against Vodafone's Indian assets and settle transaction fees.

Additionally, 19.1 billion Indian Rupees have been used to acquire 1.7 billion equity shares in Vodafone idea, increasing Vodafone's stake to 24.39% from 22.56%.

Vodafone has been gradually reducing its stake in Indus Towers over time. In June, the group sold 484.7 million shares through an accelerated placing to pay off its existing debt of 1.8 billion euros ($1.89 billion) secured against Vodafone's Indian assets.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: Bang & Olufsen Confirms Guidance as It Prepares to Accelerate Strategic Plan — Update
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping

Bang & Olufsen maintained its full-year guidance and said it is now ready to accelerate its strategic plan to drive profitable growth.

The Danish consumer-electronics company has a strategic plan to enhance its focus on the customer experience, strengthen its positioning within the luxury segment and ensure high product quality.

The company recently raised 228 million Danish kroner ($31.5 million) through a share issue, with funds earmarked to strengthen its position in the luxury audio market by increasing brand awareness, optimizing its retail network and investing in new products.

It said Friday that to ensure better and faster execution of its growth plans it is changing its sales organization. By mid-January it will consolidate its three regional sales functions into a single global sales function, while establishing dedicated functions for new partnerships and adding more local sales and marketing resources.

These changes will enhance its focus on key cities and allow it to better serve customers, it said.

The company posted a net loss of 13 million kroner in its fiscal second quarter ended Nov. 30 as revenue fell 0.3% to 698 million kroner.

Analysts had expected a net profit of 4 million kroner on revenue of 687 million kroner, according to a FactSet poll.

Chief Executive Kristian Tear said this fiscal year is transitional for the company, and the second quarter was in line with its expectations and plans.

"The EMEA region and the Americas generated positive revenue growth year-on-year, while group revenue declined marginally as the Chinese market continues to be challenged," he said.

The gross margin rose to 53.7% in the quarter from 53.1% in the same period a year prior.

For the fiscal year ahead, it still expects revenue growth in local currencies of between minus 3% to positive 3%, with an earnings before interest and taxes margin before special items of minus 2% to positive 1%. Free cash flow is expected between zero and minus 100 million kroner.

In the medium term, Bang & Olufsen still targets organic revenue growth of 8% in the three-year period to fiscal 2028, with an EBIT margin before special items of 8% and free cash flow of 250 million kroner in fiscal 2028.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: GuoCheng Mining to Offload Stake in Mining Subsidiary for 1.60 Billion Yuan
data.description:
GuoCheng Mining plans to sell 65% of subsidiary Chifeng Yubang Mining for 1.60 billion yuan, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Thursday.

Xingye Silver & Tin Mining will buy the stake.

After the completion of the transaction, the GuoCheng Mining will no longer own any equity in Yubang Mining.

GuoCheng Mining's shares closed less than 3% lower.

data.title: Airbus Deliveries Slip, But 2024 Total Supported by December Push — Market Talk
data.description: Airbus's plane deliveries for 2024 came 4% short of the company's original guidance, but the 766 deliveries it made are essentially in line with consensus estimates and the company's own downgraded target, Berenberg analysts say. The European plane maker delivered 123 planes in December, which is 10%, or 11 planes, more compared with December 2023, the analysts say in a research note. The total for last month, while impressive, "is likely to lead to a very slow start to 2025 and the increasing Q4 weighting is becoming progressively harder to get comfortable with," the analysts say. Such a push in the last month of the year has historically been followed by light deliveries in the first quarter of the following year, the analysts say. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0750 GMT - ComfortDelGro faces increased competition in 2025, analysts at UOB Kay Hian write in a note. The Singapore-based company raises its taxi platform fees to offset a new industry-wide rule requiring higher contributions to platform workers' state-run pension plans. Meanwhile, two new competitors enter the ride-hailing market, further intensifying competition, the analysts note. A bus contract currently operated by ComfortDelGro's subsidiary, SBS Transit, is up for tender. This poses a near-term earnings risk due to competitive bidding, which is likely to result in lower margins, they add. UOB Kay Hian reduces its 2025-2026 profit after tax and minority interests estimates by 2%-3%. While maintaining a buy rating, the brokerate trims its target price to S$1.77 from S$1.83. Shares are flat at S$1.44. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0747 GMT - Airbus's plane deliveries for 2024 came 4% short of the company's original guidance, but the 766 deliveries it made are essentially in line with consensus estimates and the company's own downgraded target, Berenberg analysts say. The European plane maker delivered 123 planes in December, which is 10%, or 11 planes, more compared with December 2023, the analysts say in a research note. The total for last month, while impressive, "is likely to lead to a very slow start to 2025 and the increasing Q4 weighting is becoming progressively harder to get comfortable with," the analysts say. Such a push in the last month of the year has historically been followed by light deliveries in the first quarter of the following year, the analysts say. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0741 GMT - Sky ICT is likely a main beneficiary of rising IT investment by Thailand's government, Maybank Securities (Thailand)'s Wasu Mattanapotchanart says in a research report. The professional solutions integrator will probably win IT projects this calendar year from five government agencies, including the Ministry of Interior, which have a combined IT spending budget totaling THB39 billion for fiscal 2025, 20% higher than the fiscal 2024 budget, the analyst says. The brokerage reiterates the stock's buy rating on expected 2025 core profit growth of 56% for the company, underpinned by growing airport passenger flows and government spending. It has a target price of THB30.10 on the shares, which are 0.85% lower at THB23.20. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0739 GMT - The FTSE 100 is expected to open 15 points lower, or 0.2%, according to IG. The index closed up 68 points, or 0.8%, to 8319 points on Thursday. A weaker sterling boosted the large proportion of overseas earners listed on the index Thursday. Sterling fell following a sharp rise in U.K. government bond yields amid concerns about rising U.K. borrowing and a weak U.K. economic outlook. Investors are now looking ahead to the U.S. nonfarm payrolls report at 1330 GMT for clues on the Federal Reserve's interest rate path. Stronger-than-expected data would push back expectations for another rate cut until June, while softer-than-forecast data would strengthen bets for a May cut, Swissquote Bank's Ipek Ozkardeskaya says in a note. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0734 GMT - Nordic markets are seen opening slightly lower, with IG calling the OMXS30 down 0.1% at around 2547. Markets were quiet Thursday with the U.S. partially closed in honor of ex-president Jimmy Carter, SEB analysts say in a note. European stock markets generally closed in positive territory, while Asian stock markets have ticked lower this morning ahead of Friday's U.S. jobs data. Futures indicate a tentative opening in both the U.S. and Europe. "The direction of the U.S. labor market is crucial for the Federal Reserve, and any unexpected weakness (or strength) could trigger significant market reactions," SEB says. OMXS30 closed at 2549.58, OMXN40 at 2536.42 and OBX at 1379.37. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0734 GMT - Chinese shares fell, with all sectors closing in the red, led by losses in consumer-related stocks. Investor sentiment remained weak after China's consumer prices posted a modest 0.2% growth in 2024, reflecting ongoing deflationary pressures. Midea Group fell 3.0% and Gree Electric Appliances dropped 2.3%. In the consumer services sector, China Tourism Group Duty Free Corp. lost 1.7%, while Shanghai Jinjiang International Hotels declined 3.1%. Among the few gainers, Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corp. rose 2.0% and SAIC Motor Corp. added 1.3%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index ended 1.3% lower at 3168.52, the Shenzhen Composite Index fell 2.2% and the ChiNext Price Index declined 1.8%. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0721 GMT - BBVA's reduction of the acceptance level of its $12 billion-plus hostile bid to take over Banco de Sabadell is mostly a technical adjustment at the request of Spanish regulators, RBC Capital Markets' Benjamin Toms and Pablo de la Torre Cuevas say in a research note. BBVA said its offer requires more than half of the effective voting rights of Sabadell, excluding treasury shares. Regulators saw a need to clarify what BBVA's acceptance threshold was and the Spanish bank's move effectively means the minimum acceptance is reduced by 0.7%, according to RBC. "We continue to be of the view that this deal gets done, probably by [the first half], and probably with some relatively minor remedies and a 10% increase in the offer price," the analysts say. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0705 GMT - Airbus missed its plane delivery target for 2024 but the European plane maker's deliveries should start to pick up in the second quarter of the year, Citi analysts Charles J Armitage and Sam Burgess say in a research note. Airbus said Thursday that it had delivered 766 commercial aircraft to customers in 2024, beating the previous year's figure, but short of the 770 it had guided for. "We believe 1Q 2025 deliveries are likely to be weak, due to few aircraft in inventory following the strong December," the analysts say. They expect deliveries to pick up in 2Q as new LEAP engine blades are built and put into new engines. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0651 GMT - South Korea's benchmark Kospi edged 0.2% lower to close at 2515.78, snapping a five-session winning streak. The index rose 3.0% for the week. Foreign and institutional investors were net sellers in choppy trading. Losses in battery and defense stocks outweighed gains in auto shares. EV battery maker LG Energy Solution fell 2.8% after forecasting a 4Q operating loss. Guided-missile maker LIG Nex1 dropped 5.3%. Meanwhile, Hyundai Motor rose 6.1% after announcing a partnership with Nvidia to develop artificial-intelligence solutions for future mobility. USD/KRW settled 0.3% higher at 1,465.00 in Seoul onshore trading. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield was up 3.8 bps at 2.816%. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0650 GMT - Airbus's year-end results are positive for its 2025 outlook, but engine deliveries are a key factor for the company's prospects this year, RBC Capital Markets' Ken Herbert says in a research note. The European plane maker delivered 123 aircraft in December to end the year with 766 shipments to customers, short of the 770 the company targeted but ahead of consensus expectations of 755. "We believe the key bottleneck to greater A320 [narrow-body jet] deliveries in 2024 was the engine supply chain, with the Leap in particular," the analyst says, referring to the engine made by the Safran-GE Aerospace joint venture CFM International. RBC expects Airbus to guide for around 825 deliveries in 2025, which would be positive for investor sentiment, the analyst says. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0648 GMT - Japanese stocks ended lower, as auto and pharmaceutical stocks led declines amid growing concerns of higher borrowing costs. Otsuka Holdings dropped 4.2% and Toyota Motor lost 2.4%. Fast Retailing fell 6.5% after weaker 1Q profit from China cast doubt on its outlook. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield climbed 2.5 basis points to 1.195%, its highest level since May 2011. The Nikkei Stock Average fell 1.0% to 39190.40. Investors await U.S. jobs data due later in the day. USD/JPY is at 158.36, compared with 158.14 as of Thursday 5 p.m. Eastern time. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

0641 GMT - Tata Consultancy Services is likely benefiting from some tailwinds, HDFC Securities analysts say in a research report, as they maintain the stock's add rating. These tailwinds include the Indian IT services provider's strong total contract value of $10.2 billion, driven by big deal wins across various markets and industries, the analysts say. Management's outlook of calendar 2025 growth appears to be better than calendar 2024, supported by an increase in successful deployment of generative artificial intelligence engagements, the analysts add. The brokerage raises the stock's target price to INR4,590.00 from INR4,545.00, based on 28x estimated FY 2027 EPS, above five-year average of 27x. Shares are 6.1% higher at INR4,283.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: South Korean Shares Slip After Five-Day Winning Streak; Hyundai Motor Gains 6%
data.description:
South Korean shares ended lower on Friday after five consecutive sessions of gains as investors capitalized on recent market gains, particularly in the technology sector.

The Korea Composite Stock Price Index, or Kospi, was down by 6.12 points, or 0.24%, to close at 2,515.78. The Kosdaq fell by 5.63 points, or 0.78%, to end at 717.89.

In corporate news, SK Hynix , SK Telecom , and Penguin Solutions agreed to jointly develop and commercialize AI data center solutions.

Shares of SK Hynix closed 1% lower, while those of SK Telecom rose over 1%.

In other news, shares of Hyundai Motor jumped more than 6% at market close on Friday after parent Hyundai Motor Group partnered with US-based chipmaker Nvidia to boost AI technology development for smart mobility solutions.

The professional solutions integrator will probably win IT projects this calendar year from five government agencies, including the Ministry of Interior, which have a combined IT spending budget totaling THB39 billion for fiscal 2025, 20% higher than the fiscal 2024 budget, the analyst says. data.title: One Media IP to Post Flat Revenue, Says Unit Disposal Puts It in Strong Position
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

One Media iP said revenue for 2024 was flat and that disposing of its technology subsidiary has put it in a much stronger position for the year ahead.

The London-listed digital music rights acquirer, publisher and distributor said Friday that it expects to report total revenue from continuing operations for 2024 of around 5 million pounds ($6.2 million), flat on the year prior.

Adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization from continuing operations are expected to be around 2.1 million pounds, compared with 1.8 million pounds the year before. The company said this was underpinned by active cost management.

One Media said it had completed the disposal of TCAT, its technology subsidiary, to Round Group in exchange for a shareholding in the company.

The disposal allowed the business to refocus on its core expertise of music rights management, Chief Executive Michael Infante said.

"To this end, our immediate priority will be to restore our cash reserves while we continue to assess opportunities to enhance the portfolio and deliver value for shareholders," he said.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com

data.title: Japan's NTT appoints banks for $1 billion data center REIT IPO in Singapore, sources say
data.description:
Japan's Nippon Telegraph & Telephone Corp (NTT) has appointed Bank of America and UBS to work on a $1 billion data centre real estate investment trust listing in Singapore, two sources with knowledge of the matter said.

The listing, which sources said was likely to take place in the second half of 2025, could help spur IPO activity in Singapore, where $152.3 million in IPO proceeds were raised last year, up 37.7% from $110.6 million in 2023, according to LSEG data.

The appointments were first reported by IFR earlier on Friday.

"We are currently evaluating the potential of forming a REIT. However, at this moment, no definitive decisions have been made. Consequently, we do not have any additional comments to provide at this time," NTT DATA Group said in an official email statement to Reuters on Friday.

UBS declined to comment. Bank of America did not immediately respond to an email request seeking comment on Friday.

Singapore announced in August last year the setting up of a review group to recommend measures to strengthen equities market development in the city-state.

The listing, if materialised, would also be the biggest in Singapore since the $977 million IPO of Digital Core REIT in 2021, LSEG data showed.

It comes at a time when investor appetite for data centres is growing in Asia, driven by the demand for artificial intelligence and cloud computing-based services.

NTT is a Japanese telecommunication giant with a market capitalisation of $88.92 billion as of Friday, according to LSEG data.

Its global data centres division is one of the largest in the world, spanning more than 20 countries and regions, including the Americas, Asia Pacific, EMEA and India, according to one of its websites.

data.title: Most Asian stocks decline ahead of US jobs data; currencies steady
data.description:
Key points:
  • Singapore dollar slips 0.2%, Thai baht down 0.3%
  • Indonesia shares rise 0.6%, rupiah edges lower
  • Bank Indonesia, Bank of Korea policy decisions next week

Most Asian stock markets fell on Friday, while currencies held steady, as investors remained cautious ahead of the crucial U.S. jobs report that could impact the outlook for further interest rate cuts and the dollar.

MSCI's gauge for emerging market stocks CBOE:EFS fell 0.5%, touching its lowest since September. The index has dropped more than 25% from its all-time high in 2021.

Stocks in Singapore fell 1.9%, pulling back further from a more than 17-year high scaled on Wednesday. The benchmark was on track for its worst day since early August 2024.

The Singapore dollar slipped 0.2%, while the Thai baht declined 0.3%.

The U.S. nonfarm payrolls report, due later in the day, is expected to show that 160,000 jobs were added in December with unemployment holding at 4.2%.

A much stronger increase in jobs would bolster the case for fewer rate cuts by the Federal Reserve and likely strengthen the greenback, in light of recent data pointing to a resilient U.S. economy.

The prospect of fewer rate cuts and uncertainty regarding President-elect Donald Trump's proposed tariff and immigration policies have led to a surge in global bond yields, supporting the dollar and keeping emerging market currencies under pressure this week.

"Asian markets have shown resilience due to attractive real rates, domestic support, and lack of fiscal concerns. However, we remain cautious on EMFX in the medium-term, given the potential impact of U.S. policy on capital flows and the declining real yield cushion," Citi analysts said in a note.

"We believe the USD rally and reduced EM carry make EMFX vulnerable in the near term."

In Asia, the Bank of Korea (BoK) and Bank Indonesia (BI) will deliver their monetary policy decisions next week. Both central banks have already started their rate-easing cycle, but analysts believe they will likely hold rates this time.

"Outsized FX moves in December will ultimately constrain the BoK from lowering its policy rate in January," Barclays analysts said in a note.

The South Korean won declined 0.3%, while stocks closed 0.2% lower. The benchmark equity index rose 3% in its best week since mid-November, helped by hopes surrounding artificial intelligence technologies.

Equities in Indonesia climbed 0.6%, while the rupiah edged lower.

"While BI would likely prefer to resume its rate-cutting cycle, we believe pressures on the IDR (rupiah) override the central bank's pro-growth instincts," Barclays analysts said.

Markets are awaiting inflation data from India and retail sales and GDP data from China next week.

HIGHLIGHTS:

** China's central bank halts bond buying, possibly with eye on yuan

** Japan November household spending falls as price pressures persist

** Malaysian palm oil stocks hit 19-month low in December as output drops

data.title: MANDATORY NOTIFICATION OF TRADE BY PRIMARY INSIDERS
data.description:
On 9 January 2025, Fairline Consultants Limited has purchased 1,241 shares ofJinhui Shipping and Transportation Limited (the 'Company') in the Oslo StockExchange at an average price of NOK6.1400 per share.

Mr. Siu Fai NG, director of the Company, has certain indirect interests in theCompany through his 51% interests in Fairline Consultants Limited and throughhis interests in Jinhui Holdings Company Limited. Fairline Consultants Limitedis the legal and beneficial owner of 38.72% interests of Jinhui HoldingsCompanyLimited and has direct interests in 407,858 shares of the Company before thispurchase. Following to the above purchase, Fairline Consultants Limited hasdirect interests in 409,099 shares of the Company, representing approximately0.37% interests of the Company.

Jinhui Holdings Company Limited is the controlling shareholder of the Company,which has direct interests in 60,841,240 shares of the Company, representingapproximately 55.69% interests of the Company.

This information is subject of the disclosure requirements pursuant to article19 of the EU Market Abuse Regulation (MAR) and section 5-12 of the NorwegianSecurities Trading Act.

10 January 2025

data.title: Nordex Books Turbine Orders for Greek Wind Projects
data.description:
Nordex said Friday it received an order for a total of 19 turbines from Greek electric power company PPC Group subsidiary PPC Renewables.

The orders will be used for the Rodopi, Livadaki, and Timenio wind farms in Greece, which will have a combined nominal capacity of 100 megawatts. The contract also includes a 20-year maintenance services commitment for the turbines.

The German wind power company said it booked the orders in December 2024, increasing its sales of turbines in Greece to over 1 gigawatt.

data.title: Europe before the bell: equity investors watch bonds
data.description:
EUROPE BEFORE THE BELL EQUITY INVESTORS WATCH BONDS

Global bonds have been calmer for the past 20 hours or so, but then British gilts have been closed for a fair chunk of that time - the choice of 20 hours as a timeframe was cunningly selected to avoid Thursday's UK open, when they saw a further significant selloff.

That means when stocks open this morning, even the most equity-focused manager will be keeping an eye on gilts, which start trading at the same time. German Bund yields have just another multi-month high at the open, but are only up a couple of basis points on the day.

Higher yields globally this week have been a headwind for shares, and with gilts rising more than most, domestic-focused British midcaps have suffered particularly. (.FTMC)

Then we have U.S. non farm payrolls data at 1330 GMT, the next, and likely more important, data point for bonds, and in turn other asset classes - more on that in our Morning Bid newsletter below.

Ahead of all that, European share futures are slightly lower. STOXX 50 blue chip futures are down 0.1%

In company news, the high point is Italy's Prada considering buying fashion group Versace from U.S. Capri Holdings, local financial daily Il Sole 24 ore reported on Friday.

The company is Hong Kong-listed and its shares are down around 0.7%.

Elsewhere, Sainsbury's , Britain's second biggest supermarket group, on Friday reported a 2.8% rise in underlying salesfor the key Christmas quarter.

And there's been another acquisition in the UK, where Britain's Alliance Pharma said on Friday it has agreed to be acquired by asset management firm DBAY Advisors in an all-cash deal valuing the healthcare group at 349.7 million pounds.

Lastly, industry group WindEurope said on Friday wind power provided 20% of the electricity consumed in Europe last year, but the capacity built during the year was less than half of what is needed to meet the European Union's 2030 energy and climate targets.

(Alun John)

*****

data.title: French Industry Shows Glimmer of Recovery
data.description:
By Joshua Kirby

French factory production recovered a little toward the end of last year in a glimpse of optimism for the beleaguered sector.

Manufacturing output rose 0.2% on month in November, figures from French statistics agency Insee showed Friday. That came against expectations for a third-straight month of decline in production, according to economists surveyed by The Wall Street Journal.

The country's automobile sector, which has been hit by fiercer competition from China and elsewhere, drove the recovery in November, with output rising nearly 6% on the month.

Write to Joshua Kirby at joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby

data.title: Asset management firm DBAY to buy UK's Alliance Pharma in $430 million deal
data.description:
Britain's Alliance Pharma said on Friday it has agreed to be acquired by asset management firm DBAY Advisors in an all-cash deal valuing the healthcare group at 349.7 million pounds ($429.6 million).

The 62.5 pence-per-share offer represents a premium of about 41% to Alliance Pharma stock's Thursday close.

The acquisition is expected to be completed in the first half of 2025, and the Alliance board said it intends to unanimously recommend that shareholders vote in favour of the deal.

UK-based Alliance, which markets and distributes over-the-counter consumer healthcare and prescription products across Europe, North America and Asia Pacific, is the latest among a host of British firms being bought out or becoming the target of potential buyers.

Alliance saw a change of its CEO last year when Nick Sedgwick took over the top role in mid-2024, and posted a 23% growth in its underlying pre-tax profit for the six months ended June 30.

($1 = 0.8140 pounds)

data.title: Wall Street Journal - Jan 10
data.description:
The following are the top stories in the Wall Street Journal. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

- BlackRock has withdrawn from the United Nations-backed Net Zero Asset Managers initiative, marking a significant shift for the asset manager once seen as a leader in ESG investing.

- Southwest Airlines CFO Tammy Romo will retire on April 1 after more than 30 years with the company, as the airline seeks a search for her successor.

- Elon Musk's AI startup xAI has launched Grok, its first stand-alone consumer app featuring its proprietary language model, as it seeks to compete with OpenAI and Google in the generative AI space.

- Wells Fargo has appointed JPMorgan Chase executive Ed Olebe to lead its credit card division, as the bank seeks to expand the business under CEO Charlie Scharf.

- A New York appeals court has denied Fox Corp's bid to dismiss Smartmatic's defamation lawsuit over 2020 election-rigging claims, allowing the case seeking billions in damages to proceed to trial.

- Unionized dockworkers from Maine to Texas secured a nearly 62% pay raise over six years and protections against technology use in a tentative labor deal with East and Gulf Coast port employers, reached just ahead of a strike deadline.

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 1:00 p.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was little changed at 77,676, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 declined 0.03% to 23,520, as gain in IT shares offset the impact of worries about corporate earnings.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee was marginally lower versus the U.S. dollar at 85.89, heading into the U.S. jobs report that will likely decide whether the dollar remains on a bullish path.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN067934G=CC) was quoted at 100.19 rupees, with the yield little changed at 6.7603%, as traders awaited a debt auction result.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was unchanged at 6.50%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) rose 1 bp to 6.21%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 6.20%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 6.71%.

data.title: YIT to Lead Development Phase of Turku Tramway Depot Project in Finland
data.description:
YIT said Friday that it was picked by the Turku Tramway Alliance to manage the planning phase of the Turku tramway depot project in Finland.

The Finnish construction group will lead the planning of the Port of Turku-Varissuo tramway line's depot and power supply station buildings, focusing on cost-effective strategies and identifying target and roof prices.

The target budget is estimated at 30 million euros and will be finalized during the development stage, after which Turku Tramway Alliance will decide on the implementation phase, subject to the Turku City Council's investment decision at the end of 2025.
